name: Hardener
description: Cross-platform security hardening tool written in Go — no external dependencies. Supports audit, fix, and atomic rollback from a single binary across Ubuntu, Debian, Rocky Linux, ArchLinux, RHEL, Fedora, and macOS. Published as ERNW Whitepaper.

name: Cherry Picker
description: Reconnaissance tool built during the CERN Whitehat Challenge. Leverages entropy filtering, TTL clustering, and cloud targeting for web enumeration & OSINT. Still in early development — the idea is solid, the time is scarce.

name: Forensic Log Tracker
description: Modular CLI framework for documenting and verifying forensic investigations. Implements cryptographic GPG signing and hash verification. Provides legally consistent documentation in German legal language. Used in university-level forensic training at Hochschule Mannheim.

name: ERNW Whitepapers 2026
description: Author of three security publications at ERNW: Unified Security Hardening with Cross-Platform Native Binaries (Issue 77), Linux Client Hardening Guide (Issue 76), and macOS Tahoe Hardening Guide (Issue 75, co-authored with Julian Suleder).
